name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: First-Class Honours in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, or other degree with substantial Mathematics content
summary: Combines intensive AI/ML training with practical group projects and collaborations with major tech companies. Students can conduct their individual project as an industry internship.

name: MSc Computing (AI and Machine Learning)
entry_requirements: First-Class Honours degree with substantial Computing component
summary: Deepens expertise in AI and ML with practical programming in Prolog, Python, and Matlab. Offers diverse specialised modules covering deep learning, NLP, computer vision, and robotics.

name: MSc AI Applications and Innovation
entry_requirements: First-Class Honours in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computing, or other STEM degree; background in linear algebra, statistics, and programming required
summary: Combines AI fundamentals with real-world applications across healthcare, finance, and robotics. Features a venture project with VC pitches and emphasises ethical AI alongside technical skills.

name: MPhil in Machine Learning and Machine Intelligence
summary: Offered jointly by Cambridge's Machine Learning Group, Speech Group, and Computer Vision and Robotics Group, combining expertise across multiple AI specialisations within the Department of Engineering.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: UK 2:1 minimum (typical offer is First Class) in Informatics, AI, CS, Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, or related; programming competence in C/C++, Java, Python or similar; 60 SCQF credits of mathematics (calculus, linear algebra, discrete maths, probability)
summary: Taught at the UK's longest-established AI academic centre, ranked 1st in the UK for Computer Science research power (REF 2021). Courses delivered by internationally known researchers in NLP, ML, robotics and related fields.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering
entry_requirements: Upper second-class UK Bachelor's in computer science, computing, or software engineering with strong background in data systems, AI, and mathematics
summary: Integrates modules from MSc Software Systems Engineering and MSc Machine Learning with new data engineering modules. Culminates in a research project often conducted with industry partners through the Industry Exchange Network.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence for Sustainable Development
entry_requirements: Upper second-class UK Bachelor's in a highly quantitative subject (CS, physical sciences, life sciences, mathematics, or engineering); undergraduate maths and programming experience required
summary: Uniquely combines technical AI training with applications to UN Sustainable Development Goals. Features hands-on dissertation projects addressing real-world sustainable development challenges.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence for Biomedicine and Healthcare
entry_requirements: Upper second-class UK Bachelor's in computer science, physical sciences, life sciences, mathematics, or engineering; undergraduate maths and programming experience required
summary: Combines rigorous AI methods with biomedical applications, featuring collaboration with healthcare stakeholders on real-world clinical problems through individual projects.

name: MSc Robotics and Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Upper second-class UK Bachelor's in computer science, mathematics, electrical/electronic engineering, or physical sciences; maths (calculus, linear algebra, probability) and programming experience (C/C++, Java, Python, or Matlab) required
summary: Uniquely combines Computer Science, AI, and Robotics with hands-on experience in real robotic systems. Addresses both technical implementation and the ethical and legal impacts of robotics on society.

name: MSc Machine Learning
entry_requirements: Upper second-class UK Bachelor's in a highly quantitative subject (CS, mathematics, electrical engineering, or physical sciences); undergraduate linear algebra and calculus required
summary: Offers specialisation through modules run in collaboration with the Gatsby Computational Neuroscience Unit and Google DeepMind. Features substantial industry-partnered project work via the IXN Industry Exchange Network.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Designed for graduates or professionals with a technical background
summary: Ranked 5th in the UK for AI and Data Science, this programme emphasises safe and trusted AI with industry collaboration opportunities including partnerships with companies like IBM and Aerogility for the major project.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence for Science
entry_requirements: Designed for science and mathematics graduates
summary: Combines AI expertise with scientific application, taught by world-leading academics across multiple disciplines. Emphasises real-world problem-solving through group projects and dissertation work in academic or industry settings.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: First-class honours degree (70% average) in Computer Science with minimum 50% CS content; strong programming and software development skills required
summary: Offers 75% mandatory core AI material with flexible specialisation options, housed in Manchester's historic computing department — home to the first university CS department and the first stored-program computer.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Upper 2:1 (65%+) in computer science, software engineering, AI or related field; must demonstrate competency in linear algebra or advanced maths, advanced programming (C/C++/C#/Java), and one module in AI, advanced algorithms or machine learning
summary: Combines foundational AI with emerging specialisations like deep learning, robotics, and evolutionary algorithms, with specialist project labs and flexible module choices aligned to careers in machine learning, computer vision, or autonomous systems.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:1 honours degree in Computer Science; competency in programming languages such as Java, C, Python, C++, or JavaScript
summary: Combines theoretical knowledge with practical skills through labs and group projects, offering flexibility to pursue other CS areas alongside compulsory modules and the opportunity for supervised dissertations on real industry projects.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Conversion)
entry_requirements: 2:1 honours degree in any subject; no computer science background required
summary: A conversion programme bridging non-technical backgrounds into AI careers by combining foundational computer science with specialised AI modules covering machine learning, NLP, and constraint programming, culminating in a substantial software development dissertation.

name: MSc Applied Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:1 degree in a STEM subject (IT, Mathematics, Chemistry, Physics, Engineering, Statistics, Data Science, or Biology/Life Sciences); IELTS 6.5 overall with 6.0 minimum per component
summary: Blends theoretical AI and machine learning foundations with hands-on experience through labs, workshops, and industry projects, emphasising practical applications, ethical considerations, and societal impact across sectors including manufacturing, healthcare, and finance.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:2 or equivalent in Computer Science or related discipline; essential background in programming, maths, and basic algorithms
summary: Combines theoretical foundations with practical application of symbolic and learning-based AI methodologies, with strengths in computer vision, robotics, graphics, data analysis, and games under staff with strong industry connections.

name: MSc Computer Science (Artificial Intelligence)
entry_requirements: 2:1 in a STEM or numerate discipline; IELTS 6.5 with 6.0 minimum per element for international students
summary: Accommodates students both with and without prior CS backgrounds, offering flexible specialisation pathways including AI, autonomous robotics, and human-AI interaction with research-led instruction and potential industry collaborations.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence and Adaptive Systems
entry_requirements: Upper second-class (2:1) honours degree in a scientific, technical, or numerate discipline; alternative qualifications or professional experience may be considered
summary: Distinguished by its unique interdisciplinarity between AI, cognitive science, and neuroscience, with students able to tailor their focus across robotics, machine learning, or computational biology while benefiting from leading research hubs including Sussex AI and the Sussex Centre for Consciousness Science.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
duration: 1 year full-time (2 years with placement, 5 years part-time)
entry_requirements: Minimum 2:2 UK honours degree in computer science, electronic engineering, mathematics, physics, or related fields; international qualifications and relevant work experience considered
summary: Drawing expertise from the Institute for People-Centred AI and the Centre for Vision, Speech and Signal Processing (ranked No. 1 in the UK for computer vision), with hands-on experience in deep neural networks and deep reinforcement learning.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:2 degree in a non-related science field, or 2:2 in any discipline plus A Level Mathematics at Grade A or equivalent. No prior coding experience required.
summary: A conversion course requiring no prior coding experience, guiding learners from programming fundamentals to advanced AI concepts with access to cutting-edge labs and real-world projects led by world-leading researchers.

name: MSc Human Centred Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:1 in a non-related science degree, or 2:1 in any other degree plus A Level Mathematics at Grade A. Conversion course not intended for Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, or Engineering graduates.
summary: Uniquely blends technical AI/ML skills with ethical, social, organisational and psychological perspectives, focusing on creating AI systems aligned with societal values and human needs across sectors like health and environment.

name: MSc Generative Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:1 degree in Computer Science or a related field. International students must meet English language requirements (Profile B1 IELTS standard).
summary: Combines theoretical foundations in generative models with practical applications across NLP and text-to-image synthesis, while exploring the ethical implications and real-world deployment of content-generating AI systems.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Upper second-class honours (65%+) in numerate disciplines including Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Data Science, or Economics
summary: Combines mathematical rigour with hands-on implementation using real-world datasets, integrating ethics throughout. Students may access the Isambard-AI supercomputer (UK's most powerful, ranked 11th globally) for research projects.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence for Business
entry_requirements: Upper second-class honours in a quantitative subject (Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, Finance, Physics, Data Science). Other disciplines considered with one quantitative unit at 60%+.
summary: Combines technical AI expertise with business strategy across two faculties, distinguished by high industry engagement and location at the Temple Quarter Enterprise Campus with access to the Isambard-AI supercomputer.

name: MSc Engineering with Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Upper second-class honours in Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, or Computing. Slightly lower grades considered with 3+ years relevant work experience or postgraduate qualifications.
summary: Bridges traditional engineering disciplines with cutting-edge AI technologies, emphasising real-world applications across aerospace, robotics, and smart cities, with access to Isambard-AI and teaching at the new Temple Quarter Enterprise Campus.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence for Medicine and Health
entry_requirements: Upper second-class honours in clinical fields (Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Nursing), biosciences, engineering, computer science, or related healthcare subjects. Lower grades considered with relevant postgraduate qualifications or 2+ years work experience.
summary: Interdisciplinary programme combining expertise from the Medical School and School of Engineering, Mathematics and Technology. Prepares students for healthcare innovation roles with hands-on practicals and case-based activities.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:2 or above in a numerate subject (computer science, mathematics, physics, economics, engineering, or relevant social sciences). Proficiency in calculus, linear algebra, probability/statistics and strong programming foundation required. IELTS 6.5 overall (min 6.0 in all components).
summary: Provides foundational AI principles alongside programming proficiency, with strong emphasis on the legal, ethical, social and professional implications of AI. Students gain hands-on experience in purpose-built teaching labs and complete a summer dissertation research project.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: 2:2 or above in a relevant subject (Computer Science, IT, Maths, Statistics, Engineering, Physics, Chemistry, Business, Economics, or Psychology). Experience with writing code or using a scripting language is essential. IELTS 6.5 overall (min 5.5 per component).
summary: Draws on over 25 years of experience in internationally-applied AI projects, combining real-world industry input with case studies and live briefs addressing actual business problems while developing both technical and communication skills.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
duration: 12 months full-time (September start) or 20 months full-time (January start); also available as 2 years with professional experience
entry_requirements: Published entry requirements are a guide only; equivalent qualifications may be accepted. Basic Python programming knowledge recommended.
summary: Based in Cambridge's tech hub, the programme combines machine learning, neural computing, deep learning and NLP with hands-on experience using real datasets. Benefits from industry partnerships including Intel's AI Academy.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
duration: 1 year full time or 2 years part time; both are September start
entry_requirements: A 2:1 or above at undergraduate level in Electronic Engineering, Computer Science, Software Engineering, Information Technology, Mathematics or a related discipline. Applicants with a good 2:2 degree (55% or above) will be considered on an individual basis.
summary: Our MSc in Artificial Intelligence opens doors to a thriving AI career. Explore key topics, such as agents, vision, and language, supported by leading research groups. With a strong emphasis on practical machine learning

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree in AI, Computer Science, or related field with strong mathematics (calculus, linear algebra, statistics) and programming skills. GPA requirements vary; selective admission.
summary: Combines technical and scientific AI research led by world-renowned faculty, with flexibility to specialise deeply or combine fields such as Machine Learning, NLP, and Deep Learning in an informal international setting.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree in AI, Computer Science, or related discipline with sufficient mathematics and programming background.
summary: Emphasises hybrid intelligence where AI systems and humans collaborate, offering specialisations in Cognitive Science and AI for Health alongside a human-centric technical curriculum.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Bachelor's in AI or related field with calculus, linear algebra, probability & statistics, advanced programming, machine learning, advanced logic, neural networks, and research methodology. Selective admission based on academic performance and motivation.
summary: Integrates pure and applied science through five specialisations (Machine Learning, Multi-Agent Systems, Robotics, AI Engineering, Hybrid Intelligence) with faculty from the Bernoulli Institute ensuring direct links to current research.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree in AI or related field (e.g. cognitive science, computer science, linguistics, psychology with quantitative methods). Strong foundations in mathematics and programming required.
summary: Features a strong cognitive and human-centred focus using computational modelling to understand naturally intelligent systems, with specialisations in Human Centred Intelligent Systems and Machine Learning & Neural Computing.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Selective research Master's; bachelor's in AI or related field with strong foundations in informatics, logic, and cognitive science. Specific requirements evaluated per applicant.
summary: Offers an integrative and cutting-edge approach combining perspectives from Informatics, Logic, Cognitive Psychology, Philosophy, and Linguistics, with access to specialised AI Labs and highly customisable coursework.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science, or related field with sufficient mathematics and programming. Admission based on academic record and motivation.
summary: Known for its problem-based learning approach and strong focus on reinforcement learning, multi-agent systems, and game AI, benefiting from Maastricht's international and collaborative academic culture.

name: MSc Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Technology
entry_requirements: Bachelor's in Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, or related technical discipline. Strong background in programming, mathematics, and statistics required. Selective admission.
summary: Integrates data science and AI technology with data management across nine specialised themes, reflecting TU Delft's engineering-oriented approach to building data-driven AI systems.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: Master's degree (or equivalent) in computer science, engineering, mathematics, linguistics, psychology, or related field. Prior knowledge of AI fundamentals expected for this advanced programme.
summary: A joint effort by internationally leading research units from seven faculties, offering multidisciplinary training spanning engineering, sciences, economics, psychology, and linguistics with a focus on knowledge-based technology and cognitive science.

name: MSc Data Science and AI
entry_requirements: Recognised bachelor's degree; successful completion of aptitude assessment procedure; sufficient English language skills with recognised certificate.
summary: Equips graduates to design industry-grade Big Data solutions while providing a solid starting point for research, combining practical data engineering with advanced analytics across distributed systems and machine learning at one of Europe's top technical universities.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence and Robotics
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Automation Engineering, Mathematics, or related field. Strong background in programming, mathematics (calculus, linear algebra), and basic AI/ML concepts. Minimum GPA requirements apply.
summary: Combines artificial intelligence with robotics engineering at Italy's leading polytechnic, emphasising practical applications in autonomous systems, computer vision, and intelligent robotics within a strong engineering tradition.

name: MAS in AI and Digital Technology
notes: part-time continuing education for managers, Switzerland
fees_international: ~CHF 36,000 (total programme fee, same for all nationalities)
entry_requirements: University degree (bachelor's or above) plus minimum 5 years professional experience, including management responsibility. Designed for senior professionals and executives.
summary: A part-time continuing education programme for experienced managers and executives, offered by ETH Zurich's Department of Computer Science in cooperation with the ETH AI Center, focusing on strategic AI adoption and digital transformation.

name: MS in Artificial Intelligence and Innovation
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree, GPA 3.0+, GRE required, TOEFL 100+ for non-native English speakers, 3 recommendation letters, statement of purpose, 2-3 minute video
summary: Emphasizes team-based development of AI systems for real-world market needs, blending technical AI skills with innovation and product thinking within CMU's top-ranked School of Computer Science.

name: MS in Machine Learning
duration: 16 months full-time (20 months for Advanced Study track with thesis)
summary: One of the few dedicated ML master's programs worldwide, housed in CMU's Machine Learning Department; offers a standard industry-focused track and an Advanced Study research track that prepares students for PhD work.

name: MSCS (Artificial Intelligence Specialization)
notes: can be completed online (US residents only)
duration: 1.5-2 years full-time (also completable online for US residents)
summary: Stanford's MSCS with AI specialization offers access to world-leading AI faculty and Silicon Valley industry connections, with the rare option of completing the degree entirely online for US residents.

name: EECS MEng / SM programs
entry_requirements: No terminal master's degree offered independently; all PhD students earn a master's en route. Application deadline December 1.
summary: MIT's EECS is the largest graduate program in MIT's School of Engineering (~700 doctoral students), offering AI focus through groundbreaking research alongside world-class faculty; note there is no standalone terminal master's — the SM is earned as part of the PhD track.

name: MSAI
notes: also available fully online via edX
summary: One of the first fully online AI master's programs from a top-tier university, offered at a remarkably low price point (~$10k); includes a required Ethics in AI course and asynchronous instructor-paced delivery.

name: MS in Artificial Intelligence
summary: Offered through JHU's Engineering for Professionals division, this program is available both on-campus and fully online, making it flexible for working professionals while carrying the Johns Hopkins name in applied AI.

name: MS in Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: BA/BS in Computer Science or related field, GRE optional, strong preference for 2+ years work experience, TOEFL 100+ or IELTS 7.0+ for non-native speakers, 3 recommendation letters
summary: A cohort-based program (45 students) that combines AI theory with practical application and emphasizes human-centered AI design; offers an accelerated 12-month MSAI+X track and integrates a three-month summer internship.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence for Business
notes: modular, taught in week-long blocks in Oxford. First intake October 2026.
entry_requirements: First or upper second-class degree in CS, informatics, engineering, or related field, OR 2+ years professional experience in software/data engineering. GPA 3.5/4.0 for US degrees. IELTS 7.5 minimum.
summary: Joint programme between Oxford's Department of Computer Science and Said Business School, combining AI with business strategy. Taught in intensive single-week blocks in Oxford, designed for working professionals; first intake October 2026.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence
entry_requirements: UK 2:1 (first-class preferred) in computing, mathematics, physics, engineering, or related field. Programming competence required (C/C++, Java, Python, etc.). Strong maths background including calculus, linear algebra, probability.
summary: Taught at the UK's longest-established AI academic centre, the programme offers deep specialisation across machine learning, NLP, robotics, and computer vision. Culminates in a substantial independent research project supervised by leading academics.

name: MSc Applied Artificial Intelligence
notes: part-time distance learning, conversion course (no prior computing degree required)
entry_requirements: First or second-class degree in any subject (no prior computing background required). Alternative: foundation degree plus 3+ years professional experience. IELTS 6.5 minimum.
summary: A conversion master's designed for non-computing backgrounds, combining practical data analytics and machine learning skills with real-world datasets and industry-simulated projects.

name: MAS in AI and Digital Technology
notes: part-time, for managers with 5+ years experience
entry_requirements: Aimed at managers with 5+ years professional experience; specific degree requirements not published on website.
summary: A continuing education MAS programme offered by ETH Zurich's Department of Computer Science in cooperation with the ETH AI Center and Media Technology Center. Designed for senior professionals and managers seeking to understand AI and digital technology strategy.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
entry_requirements: First or strong second-class honours degree. Demonstrated quantitative skills (algebra, calculus). IELTS 6.5 (minimum 6.0 per component).
summary: Draws on the University's UKRI Centre for Doctoral Training in Accountable, Responsible and Transparent AI, ensuring a focus on ethical implications alongside technical skills. Accommodates both CS and non-computing backgrounds.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
entry_requirements: Standard route: 2:1+ in mathematical/computational/engineering discipline. Professional route: 2:2+ with 1 year experience, or 3+ years professional experience in technical/analytical roles. IELTS 6.5.
summary: Russell Group university with partnerships with the Alan Turing Institute and Leeds Institute for Data Analytics. Features specialist modules in ML, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and modern learning paradigms with flexible professional entry routes.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
entry_requirements: 2:1 degree (upper second-class honours).
summary: Russell Group programme delivered with the Web Science Institute, offering three specialised pathways: General AI, Criminal Justice Systems, or Digital Transformation. Emphasises responsible AI development in collaboration with the UN and Alan Turing Institute.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
entry_requirements: Computer science degree (2:2 equivalent) plus 2 years IT professional experience, or professional IT experience evaluated case-by-case. IELTS 6.5.
summary: BCS-accredited Russell Group programme combining practical AI development skills with commercial perspectives. Fees are fully inclusive of all materials, textbooks, assessments, and resits.

name: MSc Computer Science with Artificial Intelligence (Online)
MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
entry_requirements: 2:2 honours degree in a STEM discipline, with prior maths/stats/computing coursework. Alternative: relevant professional experience with demonstrated programming competence. IELTS 6.0.
summary: Combines advanced technical AI skills (ML, deep learning) with critical examination of ethical, regulatory, and social implications of AI. Emphasises fairness, accountability, and transparency across sectors including healthcare and law.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
notes: fully online, conversion course (no computing background required)
entry_requirements: UK honours degree or equivalent professional qualification. Work experience route: minimum 3 years relevant IT/CS experience. IELTS 6.5. No prior computing degree required (conversion course).
summary: A conversion programme specifically designed for non-computing graduates, covering the three fundamental areas of AI: search, knowledge representation, and learning. Programming language tuition included in fees.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree in CS or cognate discipline at 2:2+ level. Reference letter from employer required. Personal statement and/or video presentation. IELTS 6.0.
summary: BCS-accredited programme covering AI programming, machine learning, and AI theory. Culminates in an independent research thesis allowing students to pursue original AI research.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Distance Learning)
entry_requirements: BSc/BEng honours degree (2:2+) in computing, engineering, or related field. Degrees awarded 10+ years ago require evidence of current subject knowledge. IELTS 6.0.
summary: BCS-accredited distance learning programme delivered 100% online by the same academics teaching on-campus. Covers advanced AI domains including machine learning, robotics, and autonomous systems.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (Online)
entry_requirements: Honours degree (minimum 2:2) or equivalent. Alternative: 12+ months relevant professional experience considered case-by-case. IELTS 6.5.
summary: Emphasises real-world case studies and live briefs shaped by industry partners. Flexible online delivery with no timetabled lectures, enabling professionals worldwide to study around existing commitments.

name: OMSCS (Artificial Intelligence Specialization)
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree in CS or related field (maths, computer engineering, EE) preferred. Cumulative GPA 3.0+. Must complete 2 foundational courses with B or better within 12 months. TOEFL/IELTS for international applicants.
summary: Described by Forbes as 'the greatest degree program ever,' Georgia Tech's OMSCS offers a top-ranked, fully asynchronous online CS degree at a fraction of the on-campus cost. One of the most affordable and widely recognised online MS programmes in the world.

name: MSCS (AI Specialization)
notes: AI specialization completable entirely online (US residents only)
entry_requirements: Bachelor's degree required; highly competitive admissions. US residents only for the fully online option.
summary: The same Stanford MSCS degree with an AI specialisation, completable entirely online by US residents. Courses taught by Stanford faculty with access to the same curriculum as on-campus students.

name: MS Artificial Intelligence (via Coursera)
entry_requirements: Performance-based admission (no traditional application required; admitted based on coursework performance on Coursera).
summary: Delivered via Coursera with a unique performance-based admission model that lets students begin coursework before formally applying. One of the most accessible pathways to an accredited MS in AI.

name: MSAI (via edX)
summary: One of the first AI master's programmes available 100% online, delivered via edX at a highly affordable price point. Emphasises ethical AI alongside technical skills with instruction from UT Austin faculty.

name: MS in Artificial Intelligence (Online)
summary: Johns Hopkins Engineering for Professionals programme available both on-campus and fully online, making it flexible for working professionals while carrying the Johns Hopkins name in applied AI.

name: MSc Artificial Intelligence (via Woolf)
notes: fully online, accredited through Woolf (European accreditation, recognised in 60+ countries)
summary: Fully online MSc accredited through Woolf (European accreditation recognised in 60+ countries).

name: MSc Applied Artificial Intelligence (Distance Learning)
entry_requirements: First or second-class bachelor's degree in any subject. Alternative: foundation degree plus 3+ years professional experience. IELTS 6.5 minimum. No prior computing background required.
summary: A conversion course for career-changers without computing backgrounds, combining flexible online delivery with practical AI skills in data acquisition, cleaning, and machine learning. Very affordable at £3,000 per year.
